steps.title: Identify the signal
steps.content: The first sentence claims Thomson's pen name "accomplished far more than simply concealing his authorship." The blank introduces the sentence that must deliver on that claim. The transition word controls whether the second sentence confirms, contrasts, adds to, or follows from the first.

steps.title: Test each choice against the relationship
steps.content:
The second sentence explains the greater accomplishment promised in the first — it confirms and elaborates rather than contrasts or adds a new point.
  • "Indeed,": signals that what follows confirms the preceding claim — the rhetorical-strategy explanation proves the pen name did more than conceal authorship. ✓
  • "Conversely,": signals an opposite direction, but the second sentence supports the first rather than contradicting it. ✗
  • "In addition,": signals a new parallel point, but the second sentence explains the one accomplishment already named, not an additional one. ✗
  • "However,": signals contrast or surprise, but no contradiction is introduced. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
